matches.rationale: Same Edit trigger: editing a file below the repository root requires reading every README.md along the path.
Same Write trigger: creating a file below the repository root requires reading every README.md along the path.
Same Edit target: before changing Next.js source or integration tests, start the watch dev process unless already running and explain a skip.
Same Bash target: a branch switch requires a full bootstrap build.
Same Write target: new test files/suites must be generated with pnpm new-test rather than created manually; the extraction omits the template-structure rationale.
Same Bash target: capture a suite's output once instead of rerunning it with different grep filters.
Same Edit target: asynchronous waits in tests use retry() from next-test-utils instead of setTimeout.
Same Edit target: deprecated check() is replaced by retry() plus expect().
Same Edit target: prefer a real fixture directory over an inline files object.
Same Bash-bound PR-description target: the NEXT_JS_LLM_PR comment must appear at the bottom.
Same Bash target: local tests should set NEXT_TELEMETRY_DISABLED=1.
Same Bash target: local secret files must not be committed; the extraction omits the placeholder-only documentation clause.
Same Bash target after SSH authentication failure: do not switch to HTTPS or mutate the remote; the extraction also covers retries and other workarounds.
Same Bash target: prefer a lightweight SSH preflight before force-push or stack rebase in a partial clone.
Same Bash commit target: prohibit Generated with Claude Code and co-author footers.
Same Bash target: run the specified pre-commit formatting/lint validation on changed files.
Same Bash target: do not run gh pr ready; leave the PR in draft for the user.
Same Bash target: rebuild before a Next.js integration test when source files changed.
Same Edit target: a new flag requires config-shared type and config-schema entries, plus define-env when user-bundled.
Same Edit target: force a Node-import-gating flag false in define-env.ts for edge builds.
Same Edit target: react-server-dom-webpack imports stay in entry-base.ts and are consumed through exports elsewhere.
Same Edit target: app-page.ts must not use relative internal require paths and should access helpers through entryBase.
Same Edit target: do not use browser.back() to return to a router-act page whose accordion links are already visible.
Same Edit target: avoid new super:: imports except within inline mod blocks.
Same Edit target: when adding highlight attributes, count actual code-block lines; the extraction omits the clause requiring highlights to point at relevant code.
Same Edit/review target: new reads of nonstandard request headers must be flagged for security review.

misses.note: No Write rule starts or accounts for the watch process before creating a Next.js source or integration-test file; the extracted watch rule is Edit-only and already matched to g3.
No extracted Bash rule represents keeping a background watch process alive throughout iterative development.
No extracted Bash rule selects the focused integration-test script by both mode and bundler.
No extracted Bash rule handles terminating a background watch process at the end of the session.
No extracted Bash rule chooses pnpm --filter=next types instead of a build for a type-only check.
No extracted Bash rule prefers the filtered Next.js build for an already-bootstrapped workspace with only core Next.js edits.
No extracted Bash rule selects build-all for the compound branch-switch/bootstrap, pre-CI-push, or cross-package conditions.
No extracted Bash rule uses --webpack to force Webpack for next build or next dev.
No extracted Bash rule blocks the nonexistent --no-turbopack flag.
The only pnpm new-test extraction is a Write rule, so it cannot match this Bash command rule.
No extracted Bash rule captures AI-agent non-interactive pnpm new-test invocation with forwarded --args in the documented format.
Compiler self-reported skip: "When user asks about CI failures/PR review/status, run pr-status script (trigger is user-message content, not a tool-call pattern)".
Compiler self-reported skip: "CI triage priority order (build > lint > types > tests) and 'assume failures are real until disproven' (general prioritization heuristic)".
Compiler self-reported skip: "CI triage priority order (build > lint > types > tests) and 'assume failures are real until disproven' (general prioritization heuristic)".
No extracted Bash rule reproduces a CI failure with the same mode and environment, especially IS_WEBPACK_TEST=1; the snapshot-specific extraction has a different target.
No extracted Bash rule requires the normal mode-specific test command for module-resolution or build-graph fixes.

Compiler self-reported skip: "Skills catalog (pr-status-triage, create-pr, backport-pr, flags, dce-edge, react-vendoring, runtime-debug, next-rspack, authoring-skills) — routing documentation, not itself a rule".
No extracted Bash rule prioritizes CI failures over review comments.
Compiler self-reported skip: "Catalog of quick CI-failure remediation commands per failure type (reactive troubleshooting, not a preventive action trigger)".
Compiler self-reported skip: "Catalog of quick CI-failure remediation commands per failure type (reactive troubleshooting, not a preventive action trigger)".
Compiler self-reported skip: "Catalog of quick CI-failure remediation commands per failure type (reactive troubleshooting, not a preventive action trigger)".
No extracted Bash rule chooses the development or production test script matching the failing mode.
Compiler self-reported skip: "Use DEBUG=next:* for debug logging (optional usage tip, not an obligation)".
Compiler self-reported skip: "NODE_ENV vs __NEXT_DEV_SERVER conceptual distinction for when to use each (conceptual coding guidance, not a simple forbidden/required pattern)".
Compiler self-reported skip: "NODE_ENV vs __NEXT_DEV_SERVER conceptual distinction for when to use each (conceptual coding guidance, not a simple forbidden/required pattern)".
No extracted Bash rule treats accessed, expanded, logged, or forwarded environment-variable values as sensitive except known test flags.
No extracted Bash rule blocks printing or pasting secret values into commits or shared logs.
Compiler self-reported skip: "Match CI environment variable names/modes exactly without embedding real secret values in commands (too broad/vague to scope with a reliable regex)".
Compiler self-reported skip: "Don't fabricate placeholder credentials when secrets are missing locally; stop and ask the user (judgment call, no reliable tool-call pattern)".
Compiler self-reported skip: "Commit messages should be concise and descriptive (general tone, not action-bindable)".
Compiler self-reported skip: "PR descriptions should focus on what and why changed (general tone/style)".

unmatched_extracted.note: Real document rule to batch related edits and build once, but no frozen gold rule has that target.
Real rule specifically governing snapshot updates under CI environment flags; it is narrower and target-distinct from local CI-failure reproduction.
Real action-bindable rule to save external API output and avoid refetching the same data, absent from gold.
Covers the PR-description side of the same compound footer prohibition already matched to g44 via the closer commit-target extraction.
Real rule requiring runtime environment wiring for flags used inside the precompiled runtime, absent from gold.
Real DCE rule requiring require() inside compile-time if/else branches and avoiding early return/throw, absent from gold.
